name: Bolivia (Plurinational State of Bolivia / Estado Plurinacional de Bolivia)
summary: Bolivia is an Andean South American mixed-legal-system plurinational state combining Spanish civil-law substantive heritage with constitutionally-recognised indigenous jurisdictional pluralism (Jurisdicción Indígena Originario Campesina). Family-law framework operates under the Family and Family-Procedural Code 2014 (Código de las Familias y del Proceso Familiar, Law 603 of 2014) replacing the 1972 Family Code. Parental authority and child custody are governed by Family Code arts. 35-49. The Supreme Court of Justice (Tribunal Supremo de Justicia) is the apex court for civil and criminal matters; the Plurinational Constitutional Tribunal (Tribunal Constitucional Plurinacional) operates separate constitutional review. The Constitution recognises Indigenous Originary Peasant Jurisdiction (JIOC) as a parallel jurisdiction. Family-law matters are heard at first instance in the Family Courts (Juzgados de Familia). Psychology profession is regulated through the Colegio de Psicólogos de Bolivia. Bolivia is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label; courts operate substantively under the superior-interest-of-the-child standard codified in Niñez y Adolescencia Code 2014 art. 12. Bolivia is non-Hague Convention.

statutory_framework.title: Family and Family-Procedural Code — Parental authority and custody
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Family Code replacing 1972 Family Code. Arts. 35-49 govern parental authority and child custody.

statutory_framework.title: Children and Adolescents Code
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Children and Adolescents Code codifying superior-interest-of-the-child principle aligned with UNCRC obligations.

statutory_framework.citation: Constitution 2009 — Indigenous Originary Peasant Jurisdiction (JIOC) arts. 190-192
statutory_framework.title: Constitution — Indigenous Originary Peasant Jurisdiction
statutory_framework.relevance: Constitutional recognition of Indigenous Originary Peasant Jurisdiction (JIOC) as parallel jurisdiction — structurally distinctive within the corpus.

apex_courts.name: Supreme Court of Justice (Tribunal Supremo de Justicia)
apex_courts.role: Apex court for civil and criminal matters.

apex_courts.name: Plurinational Constitutional Tribunal (Tribunal Constitucional Plurinacional)
apex_courts.role: Constitutional Tribunal with original jurisdiction over constitutional review.

professional_regulators.name: Colegio de Psicólogos de Bolivia
professional_regulators.role: Federal professional college for psychologists in Bolivia.

anonymisation_convention: Bolivian family-court decisions are anonymised per Supreme Court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Bolivia independence + Bolívar-Sucre-foundational framework
key_developments.description: Bolivia declared independence 6 August 1825 from Spain under Simón Bolívar and Antonio José de Sucre — substantively distinctive Latin-American-revolutionary-republican framework named after Bolívar. Foundational republican-constitutional framework persisting through 20th-21st-century political-institutional reform-trajectory affecting subsequent family-law-codification.

key_developments.title: War of the Pacific + Bolivia coastal-loss + landlocked-state framework
key_developments.description: War of the Pacific 1879-1884 substantively reshaping Bolivia territorial framework — Bolivia lost coastal Atacama region to Chile becoming landlocked state. Foundational territorial-loss framework affecting subsequent Bolivian political-institutional-trajectory and geopolitical positioning.

key_developments.title: Bolivian National Revolution 1952 + universal-suffrage + agrarian-reform
key_developments.description: Bolivian National Revolution 9 April 1952 substantively reshaping political-institutional framework — universal-suffrage (including women and indigenous populations), agrarian-reform, mining-nationalisation. Substantive 20th-century Latin-American-revolutionary framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Original Family Code 1972 + Banzer-era military-government framework
key_developments.description: Federal Family Code enacted 1972 under Hugo Banzer Suárez military government (1971-1978). Substantive family-law-codification within Spanish-civil-law-derivative framework persisting through 2014 reform-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Bolivia ratifies UN Convention on the Rights of the Child
key_developments.description: Bolivia ratified the UNCRC on 26 June 1990 — substantively significant child-rights framework integration with subsequent 2014 Niñez y Adolescencia Code substantive doctrine. Foundational best-interests-of-the-child framework integration.

key_developments.title: Evo Morales presidency + MAS + indigenous-majority-government framework
key_developments.description: Evo Morales (Movimiento al Socialismo) elected President 18 December 2005 — substantively distinctive globally first indigenous president of Bolivia (Aymara). Subsequent extended-presidency 2006-2019 substantively reshaping political-institutional framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory and indigenous-jurisdiction framework.

key_developments.title: Plurinational Constitution + JIOC recognition + plurinational-state framework
key_developments.description: Plurinational Constitution adopted 7 February 2009 — substantively reforming political-institutional framework establishing Plurinational State of Bolivia with constitutional recognition of Indigenous Originary Peasant Jurisdiction (JIOC) as parallel jurisdiction (arts. 190-192). Substantively distinctive globally most-pluri-jurisdictional-indigenous-recognition framework within the corpus.

key_developments.title: Family Code (Law 603) + Niñez y Adolescencia Code (Law 548)
key_developments.description: Comprehensive Family and Family-Procedural Code (Law 603 of 19 November 2014) replacing 1972 statute and Children and Adolescents Code (Law 548 of 17 July 2014) aligned with UNCRC obligations enacted. Arts. 35-49 govern parental authority and child custody; Niñez y Adolescencia Code art. 12 codifies superior-interest-of-the-child principle.

key_developments.title: Bolivian political crisis 2019 + Morales resignation + Áñez interim-government
key_developments.description: Bolivian political crisis October-November 2019 — Evo Morales resignation 10 November 2019 following contested election + OAS audit. Jeanine Áñez interim-government 2019-2020. Substantively distinctive Latin-American political-institutional-disruption framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory. Luis Arce (MAS-IPSP) elected November 2020 restoring MAS framework.

key_developments.title: Arce presidency + Defensoría del Pueblo + MAS-IPSP family-law framework
key_developments.description: Luis Arce (MAS-IPSP) presidency continuing through 2023 under plurinational-state framework. Defensoría del Pueblo institutional position addressing PA-related issues within Family Code 2014 + Niñez y Adolescencia Code framework. Multiple TSJ and TCP decisions developing superior-interest-of-the-child interpretation including JIOC (Jurisdicción Indígena Originaria Campesina) cross-recognition framework. Substantively significant plurinational institutional jurisprudential development.

key_developments.title: Tribunal Supremo de Justicia + Tribunal Constitucional Plurinacional + JIOC — superior-interest-of-the-child substantive register
key_developments.description: Tribunal Supremo de Justicia and Tribunal Constitucional Plurinacional continue to develop superior-interest-of-the-child jurisprudence under Family Code 2014 arts. 35-49 + Niñez y Adolescencia Code art. 12 + JIOC framework in custody disputes within plurinational-state framework. Substantive analysis without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label adoption.

structural_findings: Bolivia operates a structurally distinctive mixed-legal-system framework — Spanish civil-law substantive + constitutionally-recognised Indigenous Originary Peasant Jurisdiction (JIOC) as parallel jurisdiction. Most pluri-jurisdictional indigenous-recognition framework within the corpus.
Plurinational State constitutional designation (2009) is structurally distinctive within the corpus.
Non-Hague Convention status places Bolivia in the non-Hague Andean cluster alongside Ecuador.
editorial_notes: Bolivia jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — deepened 2026-06-09 from 3 to 10 key_developments with full Bolívar-to-Arce trajectory: 1825-Bolivia-independence-+-Bolívar-Sucre + 1879-War-of-the-Pacific-+-coastal-loss-+-landlocked + 1952-Bolivian-National-Revolution-+-universal-suffrage + 1972-Original-Family-Code-+-Banzer-era + 1990-UNCRC-ratification + 2005-Evo-Morales-+-MAS-+-indigenous-majority-government + 2009-Plurinational-Constitution-+-JIOC-recognition + 2014-Family-Code-Law-603-+-Niñez-y-Adolescencia-Code-Law-548 + 2019-political-crisis-+-Morales-resignation-+-Áñez + 2024-Tribunal-Supremo-+-Tribunal-Constitucional-Plurinacional-+-JIOC-superior-interest-of-the-child.

Mixed-legal-system Andean (Spanish civil-law substantive + Indigenous Originary Peasant Jurisdiction (JIOC) constitutional recognition). Family Code 2014 + Niñez y Adolescencia Code 2014 + Constitution 2009 JIOC + non-Hague Convention.

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator — substantive superior-interest-of-the-child analysis under Family Code 2014 + Niñez y Adolescencia Code 2014 art. 12 + JIOC framework without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label.

Joins Andean + civil-law + Indigenous-Originary-Peasant-Jurisdiction-JIOC-constitutional-recognition-most-pluri-jurisdictional-indigenous-recognition-framework-distinctive + Plurinational-State-2009-distinctive + Evo-Morales-first-indigenous-president-2005-distinctive + War-of-the-Pacific-1879-landlocked-state + Bolivian-National-Revolution-1952 + Bolivian-political-crisis-2019-OAS-audit + non-Hague-Convention-Andean (with Ecuador) clusters within the corpus.
